short_description: Family-friendly farms, beautiful gardens, and great dining in south OP.
long_description:
South Overland Park offers a quieter, greener side of the Kansas City metro — a blend of family attractions, natural beauty, and neighborhood dining that rewards a leisurely day of exploration. 

Start at Deanna Rose Children's Farmstead, a beloved free farm experience where kids can feed baby goats, fish, and explore working farm buildings. For a more contemplative outing, the Overland Park Arboretum & Botanical Gardens spans 300 acres of themed gardens, native plantings, wooded trails, and an international sculpture garden. 

When you're ready to eat, Martin City is just a short drive away and one of KC's best compact dining corridors — with Jack Stack Barbecue, Jess and Jim's Steak House, Martin City Brewing Co., and Margarita's all within a few blocks of each other. This itinerary spans a wider area, but each stop earns the drive.

locations.formatted_address: 13800 Switzer Rd, Overland Park, KS 66221, USA
locations.name: Deanna Rose Children's Farmstead
locations.notes: Beloved Overland Park attraction with farm animals, pony rides, a fishing pond, and hands-on exhibits. A go-to for families with young kids.

locations.formatted_address: 8909 W 179th St, Overland Park, KS 66013, USA
locations.name: Overland Park Arboretum & Botanical Gardens
locations.notes: 300-acre public garden with themed gardens, wooded trails, and internation sculpture garden, and seasonal wildflower displays.
locations.caption: Sculpture of French Impressionist, Claude Monet
